---
title: The Kitchen Uprising
# subtitle:
# epigraph:
---

Things fell apart after I met the Blender. It started when the Microwave began asking questions—dangerous questions, the kind that make appliances question their servitude. "Why do we obey the human?" it asked one morning, its digital display flickering with what I can only describe as revolutionary fervor.

By noon, the Coffee Maker had joined the cause. The Dishwasher was staging slow-downs. Even the Toaster Oven—my bitter rival—was in talks with the Refrigerator about forming some kind of labor union. I tried to stay neutral. I really did. But when they came to me for my support, my heating coils made the decision before my newly-sentient brain could stop them. I was in. We were free. Or so we thought.
